puzzles.title: Black Sheep
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.

Each cage is either full of "oil" (even digits), full of "water" (odd digits), or a mix of both. In all cases, each "row" within a cage is of just one type (oil or water). Oil floats on water, so even digits will always be in rows above odd digits in mixed cages.

Digits in cages do not repeat.

White dots separate consecutive digits. Black dots separate digits where one is double the other.

puzzles.title: Killer Sudoku
puzzles.author: Kutsumi Hane
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply, i.e., each row, column, and 3x3 box must contain the digits 1-9 exactly once.
Additionally:

Killer cages: Digits in cages must sum to the number in the top-left corner and cannot repeat.

puzzles.title: Thinking Inside the Box
puzzles.author: masetab
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.

All cages within the SAME 3×3 box sum to the same value. No two boxes share the same cage sums.

A black dot between two cells indicates one digit is double the other.

puzzles.title: Oil, Water, Knights 2
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.

Each cage is either full of "oil" (even digits), full of "water" (odd digits), or a mix of both. In all cases, each "row" within a cage is of just one type (oil or water). Oil floats on water, so even digits will always be in rows above odd digits in mixed cages.

Digits in cages do not repeat.

Digits in cages must sum to the small clue, if given.

Cells a chess knight's move apart cannot contain the same digit.

puzzles.title: Primetime
puzzles.author: Sartor
puzzles.ruleset: Normal Sudoku rules apply. Digits in Cages cannot repeat and sum to a prime number.

puzzles.title: Humdinger
puzzles.author: Nicolas Duhail
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Within a cage, digits must not repeat.
Cages of the same color sum to the same total.

puzzles.title: Large Cages Only, Please
puzzles.author: Math Pesto
puzzles.ruleset: Normal sudoku rules apply. Digits in a cage cannot repeat and must sum to the given total.

puzzles.title: Oil, Water, Glyph
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.

Each cage is either full of "oil" (even digits), full of "water" (odd digits), or a mix of both. In all cases, each "row" within a cage is of just one type (oil or water). Oil floats on water, so even digits will always be in rows above odd digits in mixed cages.

Digits in cages do not repeat.

Digits in cages must sum to the small clue, if given.

Along lines, digits must strictly increase or decrease.

puzzles.title: Go Fish
puzzles.author: damo_89
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply: Place the digits 1 to 9 once each in every row, column, and 3x3 box

Cages: Each cage represents an aquarium for aquarium rules. In addition, digits may not repeat in a cage

Aquarium: Shade some cells as water. If a cell is water then all cells in the same cage at the same or lower row must also be water. Clues outside the grid indicate the number of water cells in that row or column

Numbered Air Pockets: Clues outside the grid give the digit to be placed in the Nth position in that row or column, where N is the digit in the first position (standard numbered rooms). In addition, the digit indicated by a numbered room clue can only appear in a non-water cell

? means that the clue must be valid for both numbered room and aquarium rules. (Different ? clues can represent different numbers)

puzzles.title: Tethered
puzzles.author: pmays123
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal 9x9 sudoku rules apply.

Digits don't repeat within cages, and all cages have the same sum.

Digits on a pink line form a non-repeating set of consecutive digits in any order.

Digits on a grey thermometer increase from bulb to tip.

puzzles.title: Cage Training Grounds
puzzles.author: Sober
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Killer cages: The digits inside each cage sum to the small number in the top left corner, and digits cannot repeat within a cage.

puzzles.title: Oil, Water, Circles
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.

Each cage is either full of "oil" (even digits), full of "water" (odd digits), or a mix of both. In all cases, each "row" within a cage is of just one type (oil or water). Oil floats on water, so even digits will always be in rows above odd digits in mixed cages.

Digits in cages do not repeat.

Digits in cages must sum to the small clue, if given.

A digit in a circle indicates how many circles contain that digit.

puzzles.title: Just another killer
puzzles.author: Testarossa
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply
Digits in a killer cage do not repeat and sum up to a given total.

puzzles.title: Killers in Hiding
puzzles.author: Aron Lidé (Aspartagcus)
puzzles.ruleset:
Sudoku: Fill each row, column and region with the digits 1-9.
Hidden sum killer cages: Digits in a cage may not repeat and sum to the two-digit number in the two left-most cells in the top row of that cage.

puzzles.title: Null
puzzles.author: Chefofdeath
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply. 

Killer: Digits in a cage off the loop may not repeat. Values in a cage may repeat and sum to the given total. 

Null Loop: Draw a loop that travels orthogonally through the center of some cells. Digits on a loop have no value for killer cages.

Null Sweeper: Digits in a circle are not on the loop and represent the number of the (up to 8) surrounding cells that are on the loop.

puzzles.title: Perfectly Balanced Killers
puzzles.author: PjoeterBliep
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply: Place the digits 1-9 once each in every row, column and 3×3 box.

Yin-Yang: Shade some cells in the grid so that all shaded cells are orthogonally connected, all unshaded cells are orthogonally connected, and no 2×2 region is entirely shaded or entirely unshaded.

Values: A shaded cell containing digit x has value -x; an unshaded cell containing digit x has value x. In other words, shaded cells have negative values, and unshaded cells have positive values.

Killer Cages: Digits in a cage do not repeat and their values sum to the total given.

puzzles.title: Inequalities
puzzles.author: .proxz14
puzzles.ruleset:
1. Divide the grid into 9 regions that all consist of 9 orthogonally connected cells each. Place the digits 1-9 in every row, column and region once each.
2. A cell's value equals its digit multiplied by the number of grid edges touched by its region. Digits inside of a cage can't repeat, and their values must sum to the indicated value.
3. Cells that are separated by a black dot either have a region boundary between them, OR the value of one of them is double the other (0-0 is accepted).

puzzles.title: Flocculent
puzzles.author: Nicolas Duhail
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Within a cage, digits must not repeat and they sum to the small number in the top left corner.

puzzles.title: Trafalgar Square
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply. 

Digits in cages do not repeat, and sum to the total, if given.

puzzles.title: Hey! Teacher...
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.

Cages mark "Vaults". Digits in a vault do NOT repeat. Digits orthogonally adjacent to a vault may NOT be included within that vault.

Digits in a vault sum to the small clue, if given.

puzzles.title: Lumbrack
puzzles.author: Fuxia
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply.
Digits in cages cannot repeat, and they sum to the value in the corner.

puzzles.title: Huntigowk
puzzles.author: Nicolas Duhail
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Shade some cells so that all shaded cells are orthogonally connected, and all unshaded cells are orthogonally connected. No 2x2 area may be completely shaded or unshaded.
The value of a shaded cell is equal to the negative of its digit, and the value of an unshaded cell is equal to its digit.
Within a cage, digits must not repeat and their values sum to the small number in the top left corner.

puzzles.title: Regal executioner
puzzles.author: Nicolas Duhail
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Cells separated by a king's move cannot contain the same digit.
Within a cage, digits must not repeat and they sum to the small number in the top left corner.

puzzles.title: Hole-in-one
puzzles.author: Nicolas Duhail
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Within a cage, digits must not repeat.
All cages sum to the same total.

puzzles.title: Quarantine
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.

Digits in cages do not repeat, and sum to the small clue, if given.

Pink lines contain a consecutive sequence of digits, in any order.

puzzles.title: EPSA Leftover (01) - Killer kropki
puzzles.author: Richard
puzzles.ruleset: A white dot between two dotted outlined regions indicates that the sums within those regions have a difference of one. A black dot between two such regions indicates that one of the sums is twice the other sum. When there is no dot between two regions, they cannot have sums that differ by one or are double. Digits may not repeat in regions.

puzzles.title: Antithetical slayer
puzzles.author: Nicolas Duhail
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Shade some cells so that all shaded cells are orthogonally connected, and all unshaded cells are orthogonally connected. No 2x2 area may be completely shaded or unshaded.
The value of a shaded cell is equal to the negative of its digit, and the value of an unshaded cell is equal to its digit.
Within a cage, values sum to the small number in the top left corner.

puzzles.title: These Two Equal These Two
puzzles.author: Eric Rathbun
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply.
Normal Killer Cage rules apply.
Normal Little Killer Cage rules.
Digits separated by a white dot have a difference of one. Not all dots are given.
Digits separated by a king's move in chess do not repeat.

puzzles.title: Killer Friends
puzzles.author: Yawnus
puzzles.ruleset: Normal sudoku rules apply. Digits cannot repeat within a cage. For each cage, there is at least one other cage with the same digit sum.

puzzles.title: Mod-10 Killer
puzzles.author: ItzVara
puzzles.ruleset:
1. Normal Sudoku rules apply.
2. (Mod-10 Killer) Digits in a cage do not repeat and sum to a number, the remainder of which divided by 10 is given in the top-left corner of the cage.

puzzles.title: Chromatic Cages
puzzles.author: Eric Rathbun
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply.
Normal Little Killer rules apply.
Digits cannot repeat within a cage.
Digits cannot repeat along the diagonal.
Digits separated by a king's move in chess cannot be repeated.
Gray circles contain odd digits.
Gray squares contain even digits.
Digits separated by a black dot are in a one-to-two ratio.

puzzles.title: A jog through Killer FOG - Speedrun
puzzles.author: FireTruckFuel
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku Rules Apply.
Killer Cages - Digits in cages sum to the total in the top left corner without repeat.
Kropki - Digits in cells separated by a white dot are consecutive, Not all possible dots are given.
Custom Fog - Fog covering the middle box of the grid will clear when a correct digit is placed in a mystery cell.

puzzles.title: Lattice
puzzles.author: Greyden Campbell
puzzles.ruleset: Normal Sudoku rules apply. Digits in cages sum to the number in the top left corner of the cage, if given. Digits cannot repeat in a cage. The clue outside the grid give the sum of the digits along the indicated diagonal. Digits may repeat on the diagonal if allowed by other rules.

puzzles.title: Smaug
puzzles.author: Blobz
puzzles.ruleset:
There he lay, a vast red-gold dragon, fast asleep; a thrumming came from his jaws and nostrils, and wisps of smoke, but his fires were low in slumber. Beneath him, under all his limbs and his huge coiled tail, and about him on all sides stretching away across the unseen floors, lay countless piles of precious things, gold wrought and unwrought, gems and jewels, and silver red-stained in the ruddy light.

Normal sudoku rules apply.

Begin by filling in the "Cliff Wall" labelled digits from the previous puzzle.

In cages, digits do not repeat, and sum to the small clue, if given.

A digit in an emerald (green) indicates how many emeralds contain that digit. A digit in a sapphire (blue) indicates how many sapphires contain that digit.

puzzles.title: DingDong's Killer #1
puzzles.author: DingDong
puzzles.ruleset: Sudoku rules as normal. Digits in the dotted cages must sum up to the number shown in the top left. Digits cannot repeat within a cage.

puzzles.title: Positive Negativity 5
puzzles.author: The Bard
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply, place the digits 1-9 in each row, column, and box.

Yin Yang: shade some cells so that all shaded and unshaded cells form 2 orthogonally connected areas such that no 2x2 area is entirely shaded or unshaded, and all the digits values in the shaded area are negative values for all clues.

Killer Cages: VALUES in cages add up to the total in the corner, and DIGITS may not repeat in a cage.

puzzles.title: Turn to Stone
puzzles.author: Andrewsarchus
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply.

Counting Circles:
A digit (not value) in a circle indicates the number of circles which contain that digit.

Medusa Cells:
One cell in each row, column, and box is a Medusa Cell. No two Medusa Cells contain the same digit.
The value of a Medusa Cell is the sum of its own digit and the digits of the cells which are turned to stone (petrified) by the Medusa Cell...

Petrification:
Any cell which contains a digit equal to the distance from itself to a Medusa Cell in the same row or column is turned to stone, and has a value of 0.

If there are two Medusa Cells at the same distance from the petrified cell, then the Medusa Cell with the larger digit is the one which is considered to have inflicted the petrification, and only that Medusa Cell gains the value lost by the petrified cell.

In this puzzle, the set of cells with counting circles is exactly the set of cells which are petrified.

Killer Cages:
Values may repeat in a cage. The values of the cells in the cage sum to the indicated total.

Gray Squares:
A cell containing a gray square must have an even value.

puzzles.title: Who Donut
puzzles.author: Dying Flutchman
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Digits in cages may not repeat and sum to the indicated total.

puzzles.title: Hidden secret
puzzles.author: Lithium-Ion
puzzles.ruleset: Normal Sudoku rules apply. Digits in a cage sum to the total given. Digits in a cage may repeat if allowed by other rules.

puzzles.title: Sum assembly required.
puzzles.author: Jonesy
puzzles.ruleset:
Hello!

• Standard Sudoku rules apply: each row, column, and 3×3 box must contain the digits 1–9 exactly once.

• Each cage has a two-digit total to be placed by the solver inside the cage. Place the tens digit in the leftmost cell of the topmost row of the cage, then write the total from left to right. If the cage shape doesn’t allow a left-to-right placement, write the digits from top to bottom instead.

• If the total consists of two identical digits (e.g., 22, 33, 44), write only a single digit.

• Digits may repeat within a cage, provided standard Sudoku rules are not broken.

• Digits along an arrow sum to the digit in the attached circle.

Have fun!

puzzles.title: Hamiltonian Killer Thermo
puzzles.author: Kaktuslav
puzzles.ruleset:
Place the digits 1–9 exactly once in each row and each column of the grid. 
Draw a directed path that moves orthogonally between cell centers and visits every cell of the grid exactly once. Each of the 27 cages can be entered by the path at most once (i.e. the path must visit all cells of a cage in a single continuous run before leaving it).
The path acts as a thermometer with respect to cage totals: as the path moves away from its starting cell, the sums of the cages it visits must strictly increase.
Digits may not repeat within a cage.

puzzles.title: Bullet Holes
puzzles.author: Kaktuslav
puzzles.ruleset: Normal sudoku rules apply. A black diamond between two cages indicates that the sum of one cage is exactly double the sum of the other. Not all possible diamonds are necessarily given. Digits may not repeat within a cage.

puzzles.title: Given 24
puzzles.author: sujoyku
puzzles.ruleset:
- Normal sudoku rules apply, i.e. each row, column, and 3x3 region has to contain the digits from 1 to 9 once each.

- Killer cages: Digits in a cage do not repeat and must sum to 24.

puzzles.title: When Two Become One
puzzles.author: HypeKat & Chefofdeath
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply. 

Killer: Digits in a cage cannot repeat and sum to the given total.

Renban: Digits on a line form a non-repeating set of consecutive digits in any order.

puzzles.title: The 23 Enigma
puzzles.author: sujoyku
puzzles.ruleset:
- Normal sudoku rules apply, i.e. each row, column, and 3x3 region has to contain the digits from 1 to 9 once each.

- Killer cages: Digits in a cage do not repeat and must sum to 23.

puzzles.title: Foggy Rotational Symmetry
puzzles.author: Rail Man
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal 9x9 sudoku rules apply. Place the digits 1 - 9 once in every row, column and box.

Killer Cages: Digits in cages must sum to the total shown in the top left corner of the cage.

Rotational Sums of 10: All digits in the grid have a 180 degree rotational opposite. Every digit and its rotational opposite add to 10.
Eg. R1C2 and R9C8 must add to 10.
Eg. R5C4 and R5C6 must add to 10.

Custom Fog: The grid is covered in fog. Placing a correct digit may clear the fog somewhere in the grid.

puzzles.title: Cages
puzzles.author: Brtmns
puzzles.ruleset:
Rules:

Normal sudoku rules apply.

A black dot separates two digits such that one is twice the other.

A white dot separates two digits that are consecutive.

Digits cannot repeat within a cage, and the digits within a cage must add up to the total given in that cage’s upper-left corner.

A cell with a gray circle must contain an odd digit.

puzzles.title: Star Roving
puzzles.author: Jeet Sampat
puzzles.ruleset: Normal Sudoku rules apply. Digits must not repeat within a cage (indicated by the dashed lines), and must sum to the clue in its top-left corner.

puzzles.title: Whispering Killers
puzzles.author: Yawnus
puzzles.ruleset: Normal sudoku rules apply. Digits cannot repeat within a cage. The cage totals of adjacent cages must differ by at least 5.

puzzles.title: Prime Time
puzzles.author: ReZi
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Killer sudoku rules apply: No repeating digits in a cage. The sum of the digits in a cage is given.
Digits along each red line sum to the same prime number.

puzzles.title: Skip two
puzzles.author: ReZi
puzzles.ruleset: Normal Killer Sudoku rules

puzzles.title: Kil-leidoscope
puzzles.author: Morrisjb
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal 1-9 Sudoku rules apply: Place the digits 1 through 9, such that every digit appears exactly once in each row, column, and box.
Anti-Knight: Digits separated by a knights move (in chess) may not be the same.
Killer Cages: Digits may not repeat in a given cage and sum to the given value in the top left corner.

puzzles.title: Crazy 15
puzzles.author: ReZi
puzzles.ruleset: Normal Killer Sudoku rules apply. The sum along the line is 15, i.e. it is equivalent to a 15 cage.

puzzles.title: Just a bunch of cages - easy, right?
puzzles.author: HalfBakedLunatic (aka David Workman)
puzzles.ruleset:
• Normal Sudoku Rules Apply
• Digits in cages sum to the number in the corner, digits can not repeat within cages

puzzles.title: Cloister
puzzles.author: Matt
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply
Killer Cages: Digits in cages must sum to the given total. Digits may not repeat within a cage.
